_title: IEC 60909-0:2016 §3 — Fundamentals of Short-Circuit Current Calculation
_basis: IEC 60909-0:2016 Edition 3.0 §3.1–§3.7, Annex A (equivalent voltage source method)

ik_doubleprime_definition.name: Initial symmetrical short-circuit current
ik_doubleprime_definition.definition: RMS value of the AC symmetrical component of a prospective (available) short-circuit current at the instant of short-circuit if the impedance retains its value at time zero
ik_doubleprime_definition.formula: Ik" = c × Un / (√3 × |Zk|) for three-phase faults
ik_doubleprime_definition.formula_single_phase: Ik" = c × Un / (2 × |Zk|) for line-to-line faults

voltage_factor_c.purpose: Adjusts nominal voltage Un to account for variations in supply (load voltage drop, tap settings, transformer regulation)

table_1_lv_systems._scope: Low-voltage systems with Un ≥ 100 V (e.g. 230 V, 400 V, 480 V)

c_max.use_for: Calculation of maximum short-circuit current (Ik"max) — drives breaker breaking-capacity verification
c_max.rationale: Allows for over-voltage from no-load transformer secondary, normal supply tolerance

c_min.use_for: Calculation of minimum short-circuit current (Ik"min) — drives protection setting verification (ADS disconnection time)
c_min.rationale: Accounts for under-voltage from supply variation, drop on heavily loaded transformers

table_1_hv_systems._scope: Systems with Un > 1 kV up to 230 kV

near_or_far_from_generator.purpose: Classifies whether synchronous source contribution changes over fault duration

near_from_generator.definition: Calculation path includes a synchronous machine whose internal voltage decays during the fault (subtransient → transient → steady-state)
applicable_when: Generator supplies the fault (standby genset, on-site generation)
near_from_generator.calculation_basis: Use subtransient reactance X"d for first cycle (Ik"); transient X'd at ~100 ms (Ib); steady-state Xd at long times (Ik)
near_from_generator.decrement_factor_mu: Apply IEC 60909-0 §3.5 decrement factor μ × Ik" for breaking-current calculation at contact-separation time tmin

far_from_generator.definition: Source contribution stays approximately constant over fault duration (utility transformer, large interconnected network)
applicable_when: Utility / DNO supply is far enough that source EMF doesn't decay
Building services projects fed from public distribution grid
far_from_generator.calculation_basis: Ik = Ib = Ik" (constant source) — no decrement applied

near_or_far_from_generator.selection_rule: If ΣP_synchronous_generation > 5% of source Sk" → use near-from-generator method. Otherwise far-from-generator.

equivalent_voltage_source_method.principle: Replace all active EMFs with a single equivalent voltage source c × Un at the fault location. All other generators and motors are replaced with their internal impedances only.
advantages: Avoids tracking individual source EMFs over time
Compatible with steady-state load-flow analysis tools
Standard accepted by IEC 60909-2 data tables
computation_steps: 1. Identify fault location and three-phase fault type
2. Build positive-sequence impedance network from each source to fault
3. Apply c × Un at fault → compute Ik" = c × Un / (√3 × |Z_total|)
4. For peak current: ipk = κ × √2 × Ik" where κ = 1.02 + 0.98 × exp(-3 R/X)
5. For breaking current (near-from-generator): Ib = μ × Ik"

three_phase_balanced.usage: Standard breaker breaking-capacity verification; usually the highest current case

line_to_earth.description: Single-phase to earth — used for ADS disconnection time verification
